title: Apples at the Orchard
summary: A narrator describes a Saturday visit to a family apple orchard in Vermont with friends, baskets, and warm apple pie.
body: On Saturday morning my family drove to a small apple orchard. The orchard belongs to my friend Hannah. Her family has many apple trees behind their house. The drive took thirty minutes. The day was sunny and a little cold. We wore jackets and rubber boots. The grass was wet from the rain. Hannah gave us each a big wooden basket. She showed us how to pick apples. She said, 'Hold the apple. Turn it gently. Do not pull it.' I picked red apples and green apples. The red ones were sweet. The green ones were a little sour. My little brother put a big apple in his pocket. It was too big and fell out. We laughed. After two hours my basket was very heavy. We sat near the small farm house. Hannah's mother brought a warm apple pie. We ate it with vanilla ice cream. The pie was warm and the ice cream was cold. We took home four heavy baskets. My mother said, 'We will make jam tomorrow.' I cannot wait.

annotations.exampleEn: Their apple orchard has fifty trees.
This book belongs to my sister.
She carried a wooden basket of bread.
I wear rubber boots in the rain.
The coin fell out of my pocket.
The bag is too heavy for me.
She makes the best apple pie in town.
I had vanilla ice cream after lunch.

annotations.meaningVi: Tôi không thể chờ được nữa
annotations.exampleEn: I cannot wait for my birthday.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple — telling a short story
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể chuyện đã xảy ra, ta dùng quá khứ đơn: 'My family drove to an orchard.' / 'The drive took thirty minutes.' / 'Hannah gave us a basket.' Các động từ ở dạng quá khứ: drove, took, gave, wore, picked, sat, brought, ate.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use simple past to tell a finished story: 'My family drove to an orchard.' / 'The drive took thirty minutes.' / 'Hannah gave us a basket.' Past forms in the text: drove, took, gave, wore, picked, sat, brought, ate.

grammarNotes.title: Imperatives for instructions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi hướng dẫn ai đó làm gì, ta dùng câu mệnh lệnh (chỉ dùng động từ nguyên mẫu): 'Hold the apple. Turn it gently. Do not pull it.' Câu phủ định bắt đầu bằng 'Do not' hoặc 'Don't'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use imperatives (base verb only) to give instructions: 'Hold the apple. Turn it gently. Do not pull it.' Negative imperatives start with 'Do not' or 'Don't'.

translationVi: Sáng thứ Bảy, gia đình tôi lái xe đến một vườn táo nhỏ. Vườn táo là của bạn tôi Hannah. Sau nhà bạn ấy có nhiều cây táo. Chuyến đi mất ba mươi phút. Hôm đó trời nắng và hơi lạnh. Chúng tôi mặc áo khoác và đi ủng cao su. Cỏ ướt vì trời vừa mưa. Hannah đưa cho mỗi người một cái giỏ gỗ lớn. Bạn ấy chỉ chúng tôi cách hái táo. Bạn ấy nói: 'Cầm quả táo. Vặn nhẹ nhàng. Đừng kéo nó.' Tôi hái táo đỏ và táo xanh. Quả đỏ ngọt. Quả xanh hơi chua. Em trai tôi nhét một quả táo to vào túi. Nó to quá nên rơi ra ngoài. Cả nhà cười. Sau hai tiếng, giỏ của tôi rất nặng. Chúng tôi ngồi gần căn nhà nhỏ ở vườn. Mẹ của Hannah mang ra một cái bánh táo nóng. Chúng tôi ăn cùng kem va-ni. Bánh thì ấm còn kem thì lạnh. Chúng tôi mang về bốn giỏ táo nặng. Mẹ tôi nói: 'Ngày mai mình sẽ làm mứt.' Tôi không thể chờ được nữa.

questions.prompt: What day did the family go to the orchard?
questions.explanation: 'On Saturday morning my family drove to a small apple orchard.'
'The orchard belongs to my friend Hannah.'
'The day was sunny and a little cold.'

questions.prompt: Why did they wear rubber boots?
options: The grass was wet from rain
It was very muddy from a river
questions.explanation: 'The grass was wet from the rain.'

questions.prompt: How did Hannah tell them to pick the apples?
questions.explanation: 'Hold the apple. Turn it gently. Do not pull it.'

questions.prompt: What happened to the brother's apple?
options: It fell out of his pocket
He lost it in the grass
questions.explanation: 'It was too big and fell out.'

questions.prompt: How long did they pick apples?
questions.explanation: 'After two hours my basket was very heavy.'
'Hannah's mother brought a warm apple pie.'

questions.prompt: How many baskets did the family take home?
What will the family make tomorrow?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do many families like to pick their own fruit instead of buying it at the shop? What do they get from the experience?
